pasatthā.gram: past participle of pasaṁsati (pa + √saṁs + ta), here nominative plural/feminine

passa.gram: glossary artifact; passa is an imperative/stem of passati (“see!”)
passa.summary: This key is a run-together glossary line covering two words: pasatthā “praised” and passa “seeing, see”.

pasayha.gram: gerund of pasahati, used adverbially (pa + √sah + ya)

pasusaṁvāsaññeva.meaning: just living together like cattle; mere animal-like cohabitation
pasusaṁvāsaññeva.gram: masculine noun in compound, accusative singular + emphatic particle
pasusaṁvāsaññeva.summary: This is a compound meaning “just cohabitation like cattle”.
breakdown: Emphatic particle “just, only”; the ñ + y assimilates to ññ at the boundary.

pasādāya.meaning: for joy; for satisfaction; for gladdening, allayment
breakdown: Root meaning “to settle, to become clear”; pasāda is settling of the mind — clarity, gladness, confidence.
Dative singular ending, “for the sake of”.

pathavilekhūpamo.meaning: like a line drawn on earth; the simile of writing on earth
pathavilekhūpamo.summary: This is a compound meaning “having the simile of a line scratched in earth”.
breakdown: Simile, comparison; lekha + upama coalesces to lekhūpama.

patirūpadesavāso.meaning: dwelling in a suitable region; residence in a proper country
patirūpadesavāso.summary: This is a compound meaning “residence in a suitable region”, one of the highest welfares (maṅgala).

patissayamānarūpā.meaning: appearing to lean upon, as if relying on support
breakdown: Present participle of √si/√śri “leaning on, being supported”; the double ss is sandhi doubling.
Here a suffix “-like, having the appearance of”.
patissayamānarūpā.note: uncertain: the lexicon's breakdown is telegraphic; the parse follows DPD (patissayamāna + rūpa, adjectival compound).

patiṭṭhitā.gram: past participle of patiṭṭhahati (pati + √ṭhā + ita), nominative plural/feminine
breakdown: Past participle suffix: “made to stand”, hence “established”.
Root meaning “to stand”; literally “a standing-place”, hence support.

patiṭṭhāpetha.meaning: you should establish; cause to be established
patiṭṭhāpetha.gram: verb, causative of patiṭṭhāti, 2nd person plural imperative/optative
breakdown: Causative stem of √ṭhā “to stand”: “cause to stand, establish”.

patiṭṭhīyati.meaning: is obdurate; is obstinate; shows stubborn resistance
breakdown: Passive stem from √thī; “to stand against”, hence to be obstinate.

patodalaṭṭhi.meaning: goad stick; driving stick (for horses or cattle)

patta.gram: noun (neuter in the sense “leaf”; masculine in other senses)
patta.summary: Several distinct words share this form: patta “leaf”, patta “alms-bowl”, and patta “attained” (past participle of pāpuṇāti).
breakdown: In the lesson vocabulary glossed “leaf”; as a separate word it is the monk's alms-bowl, and as a past participle it means “attained”.
patta.note: divergence: the lexicon glosses this occurrence as “leave” (i.e. “leaf”); DPD lists ten homonym headwords for patta.

pattabbaṁ.meaning: to be gained; to be obtained; attainable
pattabbaṁ.gram: future passive participle of pāpuṇāti (pa + √ap + tabba), neuter/accusative singular

pattacīvaramādāya.gram: sandhi phrase: two nouns + gerund (pattaṁ + cīvaraṁ + ādāya)
pattacīvaramādāya.summary: A stock phrase describing a monk setting out on alms round, “taking his bowl and (upper) robe”.
breakdown: Sandhi consonant joining cīvaraṁ to the following vowel.

pattakalla.meaning: reached the proper state; opportune, fit and proper

pattapariyāpannamattampi.meaning: even the mere amount contained in a bowl
pattapariyāpannamattampi.gram: neuter noun, compound, accusative singular + emphatic particle
breakdown: Bowl; (the lexicon line also reads it as “obtained”).
Included in, belonging to, contained in.
pattapariyāpannamattampi.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line is telegraphic and runs into the next entry; the compound most naturally means “even as much as is contained in the bowl”.

patthagu.gram: adjective (best guess from gloss; not in DPD)
patthagu.note: uncertain: form not found in DPD; gloss follows the lexicon.

patthanā.gram: feminine noun (pa + √atth + anā; DPD also lists neuter patthana)
breakdown: Root meaning “to wish, to pray for”.
Action-noun suffix: the act of wishing.

patthayati/pattheti.meaning: longs for; desires; craves; aspires to
patthayati/pattheti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present (two alternative stems of the same verb)
breakdown: Root meaning “to wish, to pray for”.
Alternative present stem endings of the same verb.

patthayāno.gram: present participle of patthayati, nominative masculine singular
breakdown: Present stem of patthayati “to wish for”.
Root meaning “to wish, to pray for”.

pattā.gram: past participle of pāpuṇāti, nominative plural (or feminine singular)
breakdown: Root meaning “to reach, to obtain” (pa + āp + ta > patta).
pattā.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (pada paramo); only “attained, gained” belongs to pattā.

pavasati.meaning: lives abroad; dwells away from home

pavatta.meaning: that which goes on; ongoing, proceeding; the continuing round (of existence)
pavatta.gram: past participle of pavattati, used as noun/adjective
breakdown: Root meaning “to turn, to go on”.
Past participle suffix: literally “that which goes on”.
Naturally occurring, “as it goes on”, hence raw, fresh.

pavattanasamattha.meaning: able to set in motion; capable of moving forward
breakdown: Setting in motion, moving forward (action noun of pavattati).

pavattati.meaning: proceeds; moves forward; goes on, continues
breakdown: Root meaning “to turn, to go on”.

pavattittha.meaning: was set moving; proceeded, went on
pavattittha.gram: aorist of pavattati (the lexicon marks it from causative pavatteti)
breakdown: Stem of pavattati “to proceed, go on”.
pavattittha.note: uncertain: the lexicon labels the form pavatteti (pp.) while DPD reads an aorist of pavattati; the sense “was set moving, proceeded” is common to both.

pavattoti.meaning: founded on; relating to; “goes on” (with quotation marker)
pavattoti.gram: past participle pavatto + quotation particle ti
breakdown: Past participle of pavattati: gone on, proceeding; founded on.
Quotation marker; o + ti fuse at the boundary.
pavattoti.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (pavissa/pavisitvā “enter”); only “founded on, relating to” belongs to pavattoti.

pavattā.meaning: one who sets rolling; one who points out (the Dhamma)
pavattā.gram: masculine agent noun (or past participle pavatta, nominative plural)
breakdown: Root meaning “to turn, to go on”; the agent noun means “one who sets (the wheel) turning, who points out”.

pavaḍḍhissati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular future of pavaḍḍhati (pa + √vaḍḍh)
pavaḍḍhissati.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entries (pavatta, pavattati); only “grow, increase” belongs to pavaḍḍhissati.

pavecchasi.gram: verb, 2nd person singular present of pavecchati
breakdown: Present stem (DPD relates it to √yam “to offer”).

pavedeti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of pa + √vid
breakdown: Root meaning “to know”; the causative means “to make known”.

pavedhatī.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, irregular form of pavedhati (with lengthened final vowel, metri causa)
breakdown: Present 3rd person singular ending with lengthened vowel (verse form).

paveseyyā.meaning: would make enter; should cause to enter
paveseyyā.gram: verb, optative of the causative paveseti (pa + √vis)
breakdown: Causative stem of √vis “to enter”: “cause to enter”.

pavivekakathā.meaning: talk on solitude; conversation about seclusion

paviṭṭhānaṁ.meaning: of those who have entered; entered, gone into
paviṭṭhānaṁ.gram: past participle of pavisati (pa + √vis + ta), genitive/dative plural

pavuttaṁ.meaning: thrown; sown; (as pp. of pavuccati) spoken, declared
breakdown: DPD derives the form from √vac “to speak” (pa + vuc + ta), “spoken, declared”; the lexicon glosses this occurrence “thrown” (i.e. sown, as seed).
pavuttaṁ.note: divergence: the lexicon glosses “thrown” (seed sown), while DPD parses the same form as pp. of pavuccati “spoken”; follow the lexicon for the sutta context.

pavāraṇa.meaning: Pavāraṇā (the invitation ceremony concluding the rains retreat, on which monks invite correction from fellow monks)
breakdown: Root meaning “to choose, to invite”; pavāraṇā is the formal mutual invitation at the end of the vassa retreat.
pavāraṇa.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (katthaci “anywhere”); only the ceremony name belongs to pavāraṇa.

pavāreti.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entries (pavecchasi, pavedeti); only “invite, offer” belongs to pavāreti.

payatapāṇi.meaning: with pure (open) hands; generous in giving
payatapāṇi.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (payojaye “engaged in”); only “pure hand: generous in giving” belongs to payatapāṇi.

payojaye.gram: verb, optative of payojayati (causative of payuñjati, pa + √yuj)
breakdown: Optative ending of the causative stem.

payojehi.gram: verb, 2nd person singular imperative of payojeti (causative of payuñjati)
breakdown: Causative stem of √yuj “to yoke, to engage”.

pañcavassasahassaparimāṇakālaṁ.meaning: a period measuring five thousand years
pañcavassasahassaparimāṇakālaṁ.summary: This is a long compound meaning “a time of the measure of five thousand years”.
